questions.question: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort?
questions.explanation: Bubble Sort has a time complexity of O(n^2) because it makes n*(n-1)/2 comparisons in the worst case scenario.

questions.question: Which sorting algorithm is an improvement over Selection Sort?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort is an improvement over Selection Sort because it has a lower overhead cost for comparisons and swaps.

questions.question: What is the name of the sorting algorithm used in this example: arr = [64, 34, 25, 12, 22, 11, 90]
questions.explanation: Merge Sort is not explicitly shown here, but the description mentions the use of 'merge'. In the example, Insertion Sort is used.

questions.question: How many comparisons are needed to sort an array of 4 elements using Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort has a time complexity of O(n^2) and for an array of 4 elements, it requires 4*(4-1)/2 = 6 comparisons.

questions.question: Which data structure is often used with Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: Insertion Sort works well with Linked Lists because it makes and breaks the list only when necessary, reducing the overhead of swapping elements.
